behavior: Usually forages alone on the ground, walking over open patches and probing soil with its long bill for insects and larvae. Flight is fast and undulating with deep wingbeats; the crest is often raised after landing. Nests in cavities.
field_marks: Warm cinnamon-buff head, neck, and breast
Tall erectile cinnamon crest with black tips
Very long, slender, downcurved dark bill
Broad, rounded wings boldly barred black and white
Black tail crossed by a broad white band
Dark eye and short gray-brown legs
habitat: Semiopen country with bare or lightly vegetated ground for feeding and nearby cavities for nesting, including grassland, heath, farmland, orchards, savanna, wooded steppe, lawns, and forest clearings.
